rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMOND L. MAR REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Inspection Demand
rulings.Rulings: PART 1 OF 2 This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondents Antoinette Mallette and Veronica Ligne-Mar's Motion to Compel Further Response to Inspection Demands, Set One is DENIED. Respondents' motion is brought pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.300 (see Not. of Mot. At p. 2), but that section applies only to interrogatory responses. The court will construe Respondents' motion as brought under Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.310(a), the statute that governs motions to compel further responses to inspection demands. The record does not show that Petitioner's statement of compliance is incomplete. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 2031.310, subd. (a)(1).) There is no showing that Petitioner's ultimate production was incomplete. To the extent Respondents complain that Petitioner's Fourth Amended Response was unverified, the record does not show meaningful effort to meet and confer regarding this defect. The record does not show that Petitioner's representation of inability to comply was inadequate or evasive. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 2031.310, subd. (a)(2).) Nor does the record show that Petitioner made objections that were without merit or too general. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 2031.310, subd. (a)(3).) Petitioner did make objections that turned out to be meritless and unacceptable general objections in the September 30, 2019 response, but those objections were essentially abandoned after the court's January 16, 2020 order in a related discovery dispute, and Petitioner's Fourth Amended Response to Inspection Demands does not contain objections. The record shows no prejudice suffered by Respondents during the period from when the September 29th objections were made to when they were effectively withdrawn prior to the hearing on this motion. Any such prejudice, though, was attributable to Respondents' failure to meaningfully meet and confer regarding the discovery demand, including their failure to give serious consideration to Petitioner's lasy request to continue the due date for the responses. The court finds that Respondents' refusal to extend the due date was unreasonable and a reasonable approach to the request would have obviated any issue from the meritless and general objections. PART 1 OF 2

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMOND L. MAR REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Respondents Antoinette Mallette And Veronica Ligne Mar'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Inspection Demand, Set One (Part 2 Of 2 Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: PART 2 OF 2 The court rejects Respondents' reliance on Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.280(a)'s requirement that documents produced in response to a demand be identified with the specific requests number to which the documents respond. This requirement became effective January 1, 2020. Respondents' position is that Petitioner had to respond to their demand well before January 1, 2020, rendering the amendment to section 2031.280(a) inapplicable. To the extent that court could make such an order as to discovery that pre-dates the amendment's effective date, the court finds no such order is justified on this record. The court declines to grant the motion on the grounds that Petitioner failed to timely file an opposition; there is no evidence that Respondents were prejudiced by Petitioner's late filing and, in any event, the proper remedy would be to continue the hearing and no continuance was requested or, on this record, is justified. Respondents' request for judicial notice is DENIED because the information that is the subject of the request is irrelevant. (See Evid. Code, § 350 [to be admissible, evidence must be relevant].) Respondent's request for sanctions is DENIED. The motion having failed, the sanctions request fails with it. Further, the court finds that the record does not establish that Petitioner misused the discovery process as that phrase is used in Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.010. Sanctions are not available under Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290 because this motion does not involve interrogatory responses. This said, counsel and the parties must engage in meaningful meet and confer, if they want to avoid sanctions in the future. The parties are ORDERED to meet and confer regarding the need for, or wisdom of, any and all pending discovery motions, particularly in light of the court's January 16, 2020 order and this order. Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on Wednesday, February 26, 2020. PART 2 OF 2
